content.problemStatement:
You are given an array of integers. Your task is to traverse the array using a pointer and decrement the pointer to access the previous elements of the array.


Input Format
The first line contains an integer `n`, representing the size of the array.

The second line contains `n` space-separated integers, representing the elements of the array.



Output Format
Print the elements of the array in reverse order, each element on a new line, by decrementing the pointer from the last element to the first.
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Each test case consists of two lines: the first line contains an integer n (1 ≤ n ≤ 15), the size of the array. The second line contains n space-separated integers arr[i] (1 ≤ arr[i] ≤ 100).
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print the n elements of the given array in reverse order, each on its own line.

sampleTestCases.explanation: One test case with n=5. The array is printed from the last element to the first.
Single element array; reversing does not change order.

solution.approach: The problem can be solved by using a pointer to traverse the array in reverse order. We start from the last element and decrement the pointer until we reach the first element. The time complexity is O(n) per test case and O(T*n) total, where n is the size of the array and T is the number of test cases.
solution.timeComplexity: O(n) per test case, O(T*n) total.

code.Python3:
import sys

def main():
    lines = sys.stdin.readlines()
    T = int(lines[0].strip())
    i = 1
    for _ in range(T):
        n = int(lines[i].strip())
        i += 1
        arr = list(map(int, lines[i].strip().split()))
        i += 1
        for j in range(n - 1, -1, -1):
            print(arr[j])

if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()
